req.title: Data retention
req.statement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be purged on a nightly job with full audit trail.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates a nightly retention job at /cron/retention that purges expired resources and writes an audit trail, raising an incident on policy mismatch. Tests are designed to cover: (1) EP positive – resources clearly past expiry are purged and audit log is written; (2) NEG negative – a policy mismatch triggers an incident rather than silent deletion; (3) BVA boundary – a resource expiring exactly at the retention boundary (e.g. midnight cutoff) is handled correctly. Each test seeds its own data, triggers the cron endpoint, and asserts outcomes via resource lookup and audit log queries, ensuring idempotency through unique resource IDs and teardown guards.

tests.name: test_expired_resources_purged_and_audit_written
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
A resource exists with expiry date well in the past (e.g. 30 days ago)
actions: Seed a resource with expiry timestamp = now - 30 days via <seed_expired_resource>
POST /cron/retention to trigger the nightly job
GET the seeded resource by ID to confirm deletion
Query the audit log for a purge entry matching the resource ID
expected: GET of the deleted resource returns HTTP 404 or Gone
Audit log contains an entry with action=PURGE and resource_id matching seeded ID
trace: R-HLT-020: expired resources shall be purged
R-HLT-020: full audit trail written on purge
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_expired_resources_purged_and_audit_written(api_client, seed_expired_resource, audit_log_query):
    resource_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    expiry = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=30)

    seed_expired_resource(resource_id=resource_id, expired_at=expiry.isoformat())

    resp = api_client.post("/cron/retention")
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Cron job failed: {resp.text}"

    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/resources/{resource_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code in (404, 410), (
        f"Expected resource to be purged, got {get_resp.status_code}"
    )

    audit_entries = audit_log_query(resource_id=resource_id, action="PURGE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "No audit log entry found for purged resource"
    assert audit_entries[0]["resource_id"] == resource_id

    incidents = api_client.get("/incidents", params={"resource_id": resource_id})
    assert incidents.json().get("total", 0) == 0, "Unexpected incident raised"

tests.name: test_policy_mismatch_raises_incident_not_purge
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
A resource exists whose metadata conflicts with the active retention policy (e.g. policy_version mismatch)
actions: Seed a resource with a deliberately mismatched policy_version via <seed_policy_mismatch_resource>
POST /cron/retention to trigger the nightly job
GET the seeded resource to confirm it was NOT deleted
Query incident store for an incident referencing the resource
expected: GET of the mismatched resource returns HTTP 200 (resource NOT purged)
An incident record exists with severity>=ERROR and resource_id matching seeded ID
No PURGE audit entry exists for this resource
trace: R-HLT-020: incident raised on policy mismatch
R-HLT-020: error pathway – do not silently purge on mismatch
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_policy_mismatch_raises_incident_not_purge(api_client, seed_policy_mismatch_resource, audit_log_query):
    resource_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    expiry = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=10)

    seed_policy_mismatch_resource(
        resource_id=resource_id,
        expired_at=expiry.isoformat(),
        policy_version="INVALID-99"
    )

    resp = api_client.post("/cron/retention")
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Cron endpoint error: {resp.text}"

    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/resources/{resource_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, (
        "Resource with policy mismatch must NOT be purged silently"
    )

    incidents = api_client.get("/incidents", params={"resource_id": resource_id})
    incident_list = incidents.json().get("items", [])
    assert len(incident_list) >= 1, "Expected incident for policy mismatch, found none"
    severities = [i["severity"] for i in incident_list]
    assert any(s in ("ERROR", "CRITICAL") for s in severities)

    audit_entries = audit_log_query(resource_id=resource_id, action="PURGE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "Resource must not have a PURGE audit entry on mismatch"

tests.name: test_resource_expiring_exactly_at_retention_boundary
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Retention policy defines a cutoff of exactly 0 seconds before job execution time
Clock on test harness is synchronised with the server (±1 s tolerance)
Two resources seeded: one expiring 1 second before cutoff, one expiring 1 second after
actions: Seed resource A with expiry = job_trigger_time - 1 second via <seed_resource_at_boundary>
Seed resource B with expiry = job_trigger_time + 1 second via <seed_resource_at_boundary>
POST /cron/retention to trigger the nightly job
GET resource A and resource B to inspect their states
Query audit log for each resource ID
expected: Resource A (expired 1 s before cutoff) is purged – GET returns 404 or 410
Resource B (expiring 1 s after cutoff) is NOT purged – GET returns 200
Audit log contains PURGE entry for resource A only
No incident raised for either resource
trace: R-HLT-020: resources retained per policy boundary
R-HLT-020: BVA on expiry cutoff – off-by-one must not cause premature or missed purge
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_resource_expiring_exactly_at_retention_boundary(
    api_client, seed_resource_at_boundary, audit_log_query, server_utcnow
):
    id_a = str(uuid.uuid4())
    id_b = str(uuid.uuid4())
    cutoff = server_utcnow()

    seed_resource_at_boundary(resource_id=id_a, expired_at=(cutoff - timedelta(seconds=1)).isoformat())
    seed_resource_at_boundary(resource_id=id_b, expired_at=(cutoff + timedelta(seconds=1)).isoformat())

    resp = api_client.post("/cron/retention")
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Cron job error: {resp.text}"

    resp_a = api_client.get(f"/resources/{id_a}")
    assert resp_a.status_code in (404, 410), "Resource A (past boundary) must be purged"

    resp_b = api_client.get(f"/resources/{id_b}")
    assert resp_b.status_code == 200, "Resource B (future boundary) must NOT be purged"

    assert len(audit_log_query(resource_id=id_a, action="PURGE")) >= 1, "Audit entry missing for A"
    assert len(audit_log_query(resource_id=id_b, action="PURGE")) == 0, "Spurious audit entry for B"

    for rid in (id_a, id_b):
        inc = api_client.get("/incidents", params={"resource_id": rid}).json()
        assert inc.get("total", 0) == 0, f"Unexpected incident for resource {rid}"

tests.name: test_retention_job_state_transitions_active_to_purged
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Resource lifecycle states are: ACTIVE -> EXPIRED -> PURGED
A resource has been explicitly transitioned to EXPIRED state
actions: Seed a resource in ACTIVE state via <seed_active_resource>
Transition resource to EXPIRED state via <transition_resource_state>
Assert resource is in EXPIRED state before job runs
Assert resource is now in PURGED state (or absent) and audit log reflects each transition
expected: Resource state after job = PURGED or HTTP 404/410
Audit log contains sequential entries: state=EXPIRED then action=PURGE
trace: R-HLT-020: full audit trail covers entire resource lifecycle
R-HLT-020: nightly job acts on EXPIRED state to produce PURGED state
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_retention_job_state_transitions_active_to_purged(
    api_client, seed_active_resource, transition_resource_state, audit_log_query
):
    resource_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    seed_active_resource(resource_id=resource_id)

    transition_resource_state(
        resource_id=resource_id,
        target_state="EXPIRED",
        expired_at=(datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=1)).isoformat()
    )

    pre_resp = api_client.get(f"/resources/{resource_id}")
    assert pre_resp.status_code == 200
    assert pre_resp.json()["state"] == "EXPIRED", "Pre-condition: resource must be EXPIRED"

    cron_resp = api_client.post("/cron/retention")
    assert cron_resp.status_code == 200

    post_resp = api_client.get(f"/resources/{resource_id}")
    assert post_resp.status_code in (200, 404, 410)
    if post_resp.status_code == 200:
        assert post_resp.json()["state"] == "PURGED"

    entries = audit_log_query(resource_id=resource_id)
    actions = [e["action"] for e in entries]
    assert "EXPIRE" in actions or "STATE_CHANGE" in actions, "Missing EXPIRE audit entry"
    assert "PURGE" in actions, "Missing PURGE audit entry"
    purge_idx = next(i for i, e in enumerate(entries) if e["action"] == "PURGE")
    expire_idx = next(i for i, e in enumerate(entries) if e["action"] in ("EXPIRE", "STATE_CHANGE"))
    assert expire_idx < purge_idx, "EXPIRE must precede PURGE in audit log"
